claim_scope: Sales module maintenance slice for SX-2499 import feedback bugfix

subissue_fix_mapping.2.1_multi_sheet: parseImportOrders scans all sheets and selects the one whose header row matches orderImportHeaders. Errors on 0 or >1 matches. Covered by direct tests for blank Sheet1, zero matches, and multiple matches. ExportTemplate produces a single sheet named 'Order Template'.
subissue_fix_mapping.2.2_document_date: parseImportDate accepts Excel serial, dd/MM/yyyy, yyyy-MM-dd, and strips a leading apostrophe for backward compat. ExportTemplate sample B2 styled as date (numFmtId=14).
subissue_fix_mapping.2.3_optional_fields: ProCode, Promo, Discount, PPN are optional. parseOptionalNumber returns 0 on blank and invalid-number error otherwise. Blank ProCode skips product lookup and sets detail.ProId=0 (entity field is int, not pointer). Covered by direct tests for blank ProCode and blank Promo/Discount/PPN.
subissue_fix_mapping.2.4_salesman_lookup: FindSalesmanByCode trims input and queries salesman_code against mst.m_salesman. Covered by direct tests for trimmed and not-found SalesmanCode paths.

deviations_from_plan: excelize v2.9.1 Style struct does not expose QuotePrefix at Go API level; the date numFmtId=14 is the critical part and was applied. quotePrefix attribute is only needed for date-looking text, not for typed date values.

entity.CreateOrderDetBody.ProId is int (not *int), so the plan's ProId=nil was implemented as ProId=0 for blank ProCode rows. If sls.order_detail.pro_id has a foreign key rejecting 0, a schema/follow-up will be needed. This remains unverified at runtime/DB layer.

residual_risks.risk: sls.order_detail.pro_id FK may reject 0 for blank ProCode rows; no quick non-destructive local DB fixture was available in this task context to verify persistence safety.
FindSalesmanByCode remains case-sensitive by design, matching existing lookup style in this module.
